attacking_style: Capable of extremely long-range solo attacks (48km) with sustained power, but also patient finisher from reduced groups, striking in final 300m when positioned correctly.
team_tactics: Astana leverages numerical advantage to control small elite groups through passive presence rather than aggressive pacing, forcing rival hesitation while Valgren conserves energy.
terrain_preference: Cobbled classics with multiple climbs suit his rare endurance/power combination best; less dependent on traditional puncheur finishes like Cauberg, thrives on varied/unpredictable parcours.
vulnerability: Limited observations suggest reliance on either breakaway situations or team numerical advantage; unclear how he performs when isolated in pure sprint scenarios from larger groups.
key_patterns: Won from both extreme long-range solo (48km) and late tactical strike (300m), showing dual threat capability.
Team numerical superiority creates tactical paralysis among rivals, enabling calculated patience rather than forced aggression.
Breakthrough classics results (Omloop, Amstel wins) demonstrate sustained elite form across cobbles and hilly one-day races.
Strong support role in stage races (Tour top-5 while working) indicates versatility beyond pure classics specialization.
form_trajectory: positive: Two major classics wins (Omloop, Amstel) early 2018 followed by strong Tour support role suggests breakthrough season with sustained form across race types
